name: Butchered Snake Bits with Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a large saucepan, colander, platter, carrot peeler, knife, and toothpicks.
2. With an adult's help, cook the pasta according to the package directions.
3. Carefully drain the cooked pasta through a colander over the sink.
4. Rinse the pasta in cold water and drain again.
5. To make snakes, cover one end of each rigatoni with your finger to prevent leakage.
Carefully fill each piece of pasta with cheese spread.
6. Place six to eight cheese-filled rigatonis end to end on a serving platter,
forming a realistically curvy snake shape.
7. Using a toothpick, spread lines of barbecue sauce along the top of each snake
for markings.
8. To form heads, use barbecue sauce to glue two black peppercorn eyes onto the
end opposite the tail of each snake.
9. Wash, dry, and carefully peel the skin off the carrot.
10. When completely clean of skin, make one more peeling for each snake formed.
11. At the narrow end of each peel, carefully cut out a long, thin triangle.
These are your snakes' forked tongues.
12. For serving suggestion, assemble two or three snakes lying on their backs,
as if they died in agony. Belly-up snakes have no markings drawn on them,
as snake bellies are generally a solid color. Allow one or two sidewinders
to wriggle over the edge of your plate.
